channel.title: AI Red Team Log
channel.description: Recent content on AI Red Team Log

item.title: Prompt Injection: a working taxonomy
item.description: My own reference notes on the different flavors of prompt injection and where each shows up in real systems.

item.title: Vulnerable Agent Lab: attacking a tool-using LLM agent
item.description: Building a deliberately vulnerable LangChain agent with file and email tools, then attacking it with indirect prompt injection.

item.title: Week 1: OWASP LLM Top 10 and setting up a local sandbox
item.description: Starting the AI red teaming track. Read through OWASP’s Top 10 for LLM Applications and set up a local LLM + RAG environment to test against.

item.title: About
item.description:
Hi, I’m Lynne

I work in AI infra and sales, and I’m currently pursuing the Cybersecurity specialization in Georgia Tech’s OMSCS program.

I’m transitioning toward AI Red Teaming — evaluating the security of LLM-based systems, RAG pipelines, and agentic workflows through an attacker’s lens. This site documents that journey: weekly learning logs, hands-on projects, and technical notes.

Background

  • OMSCS coursework: Network Security (CS 6262), Information Security (CS 6035), Secure Computer Systems (CS 6238), Software Engineering (CS 6300)
  • Hands-on experience: Snort IDS rule development, penetration testing (Metasploit, John the Ripper, HTTP request smuggling), web security (XSS/CSRF/CORS), adversarial ML (PAYL-based evasion)
  • Currently learning: prompt injection, RAG pipeline security, AI red team tooling (Garak, PyRIT), Go

Find me

  • GitHub:https://github.com/LynneYao666
  • LinkedIn:https://www.linkedin.com/in/lynne-y-520349185/
  • Email: lyaolynne7@gmail.com
